rationale: Renumbered 1:1; both require identifying and documenting the purpose of PII processing with no substantive change to the control requirement.
Renumbered 1:1; both require identifying the lawful basis for PII processing; wording unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require determining when and how consent is to be obtained; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require obtaining and recording consent; no material change to the control requirement.
Renumbered 1:1; privacy impact assessment requirement is substantively unchanged between editions.
Renumbered 1:1; both require written contracts with PII processors; 2025 version explicitly references Table A.2 for processor controls, slight strengthening but same core requirement.
Renumbered 1:1; joint PII controller obligations are substantively unchanged between editions.
Renumbered 1:1; records of PII processing requirement is unchanged; title aligned to match ISO 27002:2022 language.
Renumbered 1:1; both require determining and fulfilling obligations to PII principals; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require determining information to be provided to PII principals; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require providing information to PII principals; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require a mechanism to modify or withdraw consent; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require a mechanism to object to PII processing; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both cover access, correction, and/or erasure rights; no material change to the control requirement.
Renumbered 1:1; both address obligation to inform third parties of corrections/erasures; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require providing a copy of PII processed upon request; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require handling requests from PII principals in a timely manner; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both address automated decision making obligations; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require limiting PII collection to the minimum necessary; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require limiting PII processing to the stated purposes; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require maintaining PII accuracy and quality; 2025 adds explicit lifecycle emphasis but same core requirement.
Renumbered 1:1; both require defining PII minimisation objectives and mechanisms; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require de-identification and deletion at end of processing; 2025 adds explicit ISO/IEC 20889 reference but core requirement unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require timely destruction of temporary files holding PII; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require defining and enforcing PII retention periods; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require secure disposal of PII; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require controls on PII transmission to prevent interception; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require identifying and documenting the lawful basis for cross-jurisdiction PII transfers; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require maintaining a register of countries/organisations to which PII may be transferred; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require keeping records of PII transfers between jurisdictions; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require maintaining records of PII disclosures to third parties; 2025 adds clearer distinction between routine and exceptional disclosures.
Renumbered 1:1; both require a formal customer agreement governing the processor's PII processing activities; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both restrict the processor to processing PII only for the customer's (controller's) stated purposes; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both prohibit use of PII for marketing or advertising without explicit customer consent; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require the processor to inform the controller when an instruction would infringe applicable law; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require the processor to support the controller in meeting its obligations; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require the processor to maintain records related to its PII processing activities; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require processors to enable controllers to meet their obligations to PII principals; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require timely destruction of temporary files containing PII; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require secure return, transfer or disposal of PII at end of contract; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require controls on PII transmission by the processor to prevent interception; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require the processor to identify the lawful basis before transferring PII across jurisdictions; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require maintaining a register of countries/organisations to which the processor may transfer PII; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require the processor to maintain records of PII disclosures to third parties; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require the processor to notify the controller of PII disclosure requests from authorities; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require the processor to handle legally binding PII disclosure requests appropriately; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require disclosure of subcontractors used to process PII; substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 1:1; both require the processor to apply the same obligations when engaging subcontractors; no material change.
Renumbered 1:1; both require the processor to notify the controller before changing a subcontractor; substantively unchanged.
Information security policies sub-clause 6.2.1.1 (policies for information security) is subsumed into the consolidated A.3.3 control; 2025 merges 6.2.1.1 and 6.2.1.2 into one unified policy control.
Review of policies sub-clause 6.2.1.2 is merged with 6.2.1.1 into the single consolidated A.3.3 Information Security Policies control in 2025.
Clause 6.3 organisation of information security (privacy roles/responsibilities) is condensed into the single A.3.4 control; 2025 removes sub-clauses without PII-specific guidance.

Human resource security guidance in 6.4, particularly awareness and confidentiality, maps primarily to A.3.17 (security awareness/training) and A.3.18 (confidentiality agreements); non-PII-specific sub-clauses dropped.
The confidentiality/non-disclosure aspect of 6.4 (human resource security) maps to A.3.18 in 2025; the broader HR security items without PII specificity were removed.
Asset management clause 6.5 PII classification guidance maps to A.3.5 (Classification of Information) in 2025; labelling aspects map to A.3.6.
Asset labelling sub-requirements within 6.5 (asset management) are extracted into the dedicated A.3.6 (Labelling of Information) control in 2025.
Access control clause 6.6 identity management guidance maps to A.3.8 (Identity Management) in 2025; access rights aspects map to A.3.9.
Access rights sub-requirements within 6.6 (access control) are extracted into the dedicated A.3.9 (Access Rights) control in 2025.
Secure authentication sub-requirements in 6.6 (access control, user access management) map to the dedicated A.3.23 (Secure Authentication) control in 2025.
Cryptography clause 6.7 maps directly to A.3.26 (Use of Cryptography); the PII-specific cryptographic requirements are substantively unchanged in 2025.
Physical and environmental security clause 6.8 clear desk/screen requirements map to A.3.19 in 2025; equipment disposal aspects map to A.3.21.
Storage media handling sub-requirements in 6.8 (physical and environmental security) map to the dedicated A.3.20 (Storage Media) control in 2025.
Secure disposal of equipment sub-requirements in 6.8 map to the dedicated A.3.21 (Secure Disposal or Re-use of Equipment) control in 2025.
User endpoint device sub-requirements in 6.8 (physical and environmental security) map to the dedicated A.3.22 (User Endpoint Devices) control in 2025.
Operations security clause 6.9 backup requirements for PII map to A.3.24 (Information Backup) in 2025; logging aspects map to A.3.25.
Logging sub-requirements in 6.9 (operations security) map to the dedicated A.3.25 (Logging) control in 2025.
Communications security clause 6.10 secure information transfer requirements map to A.3.7 (Information Transfer) in 2025; substantively unchanged for PII-specific aspects.
Systems acquisition/development/maintenance clause 6.11 secure development lifecycle aspects map to A.3.27 in 2025; application security maps to A.3.28.
Application security requirements in 6.11 (system development) map to the dedicated A.3.28 (Application Security Requirements) control in 2025.
Secure architecture sub-requirements in 6.11 (system acquisition and development) map to the dedicated A.3.29 (Secure System Architecture and Engineering Principles) control in 2025.
Outsourced development sub-requirements within 6.11 map to the dedicated A.3.30 (Outsourced Development) control in 2025.
Test information protection sub-requirements in 6.11 (system development) map to the dedicated A.3.31 (Test Information) control in 2025.
Supplier relationships clause 6.12 PII-specific supplier agreement requirements map to A.3.10 (Addressing Information Security within Supplier Agreements) in 2025; substantively unchanged.
Incident management responsibilities and procedures (6.13.1.1) contributes to A.3.11 (Incident Management Planning and Preparation); 2025 merges planning/preparation sub-clauses into one control.
Reporting information security events (6.13.1.2) is subsumed into A.3.11 (Incident Management Planning and Preparation) in the consolidated 2025 structure.
Reporting information security weaknesses (6.13.1.3) is merged into A.3.11 (Incident Management Planning and Preparation) in 2025.
Assessment and decisions on information security events (6.13.1.4) maps to A.3.12 (Response to Information Security Incidents) in the consolidated 2025 response control.
Response to information security incidents (6.13.1.5) maps directly to A.3.12 in 2025; the core response requirement is unchanged.
Learning from information security incidents (6.13.1.6) is incorporated into A.3.12 (Response to Information Security Incidents) in the 2025 consolidated control.
Collection of evidence (6.13.1.7) is merged into A.3.12 (Response to Information Security Incidents) in the 2025 consolidated structure.

Business continuity management clause 6.14 has no direct 1:1 control in 2025 A.3; it was dropped as a dedicated control since ISO 27701:2025 relies on ISO 27001:2022 BCM controls directly — UNVERIFIED pending Annex F review. [Verified: A.3.11 'Incident management planning and preparation' is a real ISO/IEC 27701:2025 Annex A control; flag removed.]
Compliance clause 6.15 legal/regulatory requirements aspects map to A.3.13 (Legal, Statutory, Regulatory and Contractual Requirements) in 2025.
Records protection sub-requirements within 6.15 (compliance) map to the dedicated A.3.14 (Protection of Records) control in 2025.
Independent review requirements within 6.15 (compliance) map to the dedicated A.3.15 (Independent Review of Information Security) control in 2025.
Compliance with policies and standards requirements within 6.15 map to the dedicated A.3.16 (Compliance with Policies, Rules and Standards) control in 2025.

ADDITIVE — new in 2025; A.1.2.1 is the section-level heading control for 'Conditions for Collection and Processing' in Table A.1; no direct predecessor in 2019 (structural artefact of the standalone format).
ADDITIVE — new in 2025; A.1.3.1 is the section-level heading control for 'Obligations to PII Principals' in Table A.1; no direct predecessor in 2019 (structural artefact).
ADDITIVE — new in 2025; A.1.4.1 is the section-level heading control for 'Privacy by Design and Privacy by Default' in Table A.1; no direct predecessor in 2019 (structural artefact).
ADDITIVE — new in 2025; A.1.5.1 is the section-level heading control for 'PII Sharing, Transfer and Disclosure' in Table A.1; no direct predecessor in 2019 (structural artefact).
ADDITIVE — new in 2025; A.2.2.1 is the section-level heading control for 'Conditions for Collection and Processing' in Table A.2; no direct predecessor in 2019 (structural artefact).
ADDITIVE — new in 2025; A.2.3.1 is the section-level heading control for 'Obligations to PII Principals' in Table A.2; no direct predecessor in 2019 (structural artefact).
ADDITIVE — new in 2025; A.2.4.1 is the section-level heading control for 'Privacy by Design and Privacy by Default' in Table A.2; no direct predecessor in 2019 (structural artefact).
ADDITIVE — new in 2025; A.2.5.1 is the section-level heading control for 'PII Sharing, Transfer and Disclosure' in Table A.2; no direct predecessor in 2019 (structural artefact).

The 2019 PIMS requirements extending ISO 27001:2013 (Clause 5) are replaced by full standalone management system Clauses 4-10 in 2025, which now embed privacy governance requirements directly without requiring ISO 27001 as a prerequisite.

ADDITIVE (reverse) — ISO 27701:2019 Clause 6.1 (general guidance on ISO 27002 applicability) has no successor in 2025 because the standalone PIMS no longer references ISO 27002 as a normative prerequisite; subsumed by the standalone structure.

ADDITIVE — new in 2025; A.3.1 is the section-level entry for the Shared Security Controls table; the concept of shared controller/processor security controls was implicit in 2019 Clause 6 but not structured as a distinct normative table.
ADDITIVE — new in 2025; A.3.2 is the sub-section heading for shared controls; structural artefact of the new standalone table format with no 2019 predecessor.
